subject: Project Management
title: Construction and Civil Engineering
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Construction and Civil Engineering.

hook.text: You can't build a skyscraper with sprints. Construction has used waterfall successfully for thousands of years.

buildup.text: Blueprints come first, permits second, foundation third, structure fourth. The sequence is non-negotiable.

discovery.text: In construction, rework is catastrophic: you can't un-pour concrete or un-weld steel beams.

twist.text: Even construction is adopting some agile ideas: modular building, prefabrication, and iterative design reviews.

climax.text: Waterfall thrives where physical constraints make change expensive and requirements are well understood.

punchline.text: When you can't undo it, plan it right the first time.

quiz.question: Why does waterfall work well in construction?
options: Physical work is hard to undo, so upfront planning is essential
It's the only legal method for building
